questions.text: When I see that something can be improved, I usually...
options: Wait until I fully understand how to make it better.
Discuss with others whether something should be changed.
Try to make an improvement right away, even a small one.
Notice what could be better but avoid getting involved unless asked.

questions.text: When a group discussion reaches a dead end and no one suggests ideas, I...
options: Pause and wait for someone else to speak first.
Ask a question to restart the discussion.
Offer an idea, even if it's not fully developed.
Suggest postponing the discussion until later.

questions.text: When I face a problem I've never encountered before, I...
options: Look for a solution on my own, using available resources.
First learn how others have solved similar problems.
Ask someone who knows better.
Leave it as it is to avoid making things worse.

questions.text: If a teammate makes a mistake that affects my part of the project, I...
options: Discuss it with them and help fix it since it's a shared outcome.
Inform the supervisor and let them decide what to do.
Mention the mistake but avoid interfering because everyone is responsible for their part.
Point out that my part was done correctly and it's not my responsibility.

questions.text: If I promised to help someone but realize I won't make it on time, I...
options: Let them know I can't help and explain why.
Inform them early, apologize, and try to fulfill my promise later.
Avoid contact for a while to skip awkward explanations.
Wait until they solve it on their own.

questions.text: If I promised the team to complete a task but realize I can't, I...
options: Inform them in advance and help reassign the task.
Stay silent, hoping to finish at least part of it.
Explain later that my situation changed and it's not my fault.
Warn the team at the last moment and ask for their help.

questions.text: When I'm learning something new and face difficulties, I...
options: See it as part of growth and look for solutions.
Stop doing it, thinking it's just not for me.
Put it aside and return later when inspired.
Ask for help to save time.

questions.text: When I think about my future, I...
options: Plan which skills to develop to move forward.
Go with the flow because life will show the way.
Listen to advice and try to define a direction for growth.
Don't think about it much because luck decides most things.

questions.text: When I notice the teacher explains something superficially, I...
options: Assume it's not that important.
Ask for additional reading materials.
Wait, hoping the next class will clarify things.
Find extra sources and study the topic deeper on my own.

questions.text: When a task takes more time than expected, I...
options: Adjust my approach and continue until I finish.
Switch to something else to avoid getting stuck.
Wait until I get more support or resources.
Break it down into smaller parts to move gradually.

questions.text: When I lack motivation or energy, I...
options: Remind myself why I started and finish anyway.
Switch to easier parts to maintain momentum.
Stop, believing that forcing myself won't help.
Take a break and wait for the right mood.

questions.text: When competition rules suddenly change at the final stage, I...
options: Complete it formally because it's too late to change much.
Keep the same approach but improve the presentation.
Adapt my project to the new requirements.
Drop out, believing it's unfair.

questions.text: When I'm praised for good work, I...
options: Take it as a sign to aim even higher.
Feel happy and think about maintaining this level.
Thank them and keep doing the same.
Feel embarrassed and try not to stand out again.

questions.text: When I'm offered a challenging, high-risk task where I could shine, I...
options: Avoid such situations.
Weigh the risks and prepare to minimize errors.
Wait for someone else to take it first.
Take it on because the chance to prove myself is worth the risk.

questions.text: When I imagine my ideal career path, I see myself...
options: In a position where my decisions make a real impact.
As an expert whose opinion is respected.
In a stable job with little responsibility.
In a calm job without pressure to prove myself.

questions.text: When I see injustice, but speaking up might complicate my life, I...
options: Prefer not to get involved to avoid trouble.
Still speak up, because it's hard for me to stay silent when someone is hurt.
Think it's not my business since I can't change anything anyway.
Try at least to talk to the person affected.

questions.text: If I notice someone takes my help for granted, I...
options: Try to explain that my help is a choice, not an obligation.
Feel irritated and cut off contact completely.
Distance myself, as I don't want to be taken advantage of.
Keep helping anyway because I don't do it for gratitude.

questions.text: When I see someone doesn't have the materials for an exam, I...
options: Pretend not to notice, otherwise they'll keep asking.
Share mine because we're learning together after all.
Tell them where they can find the materials.
Think everyone should take care of themselves.

questions.text: If, during an internship, I see that the supervisor uses students as free labor, I...
options: Gather opinions from other interns and prepare a constructive message to management together.
Agree with others that everyone does that and there is no point trying to change anything.
Prefer to finish my internship quietly and avoid unnecessary conflicts.
Try to have a calm, honest conversation with the supervisor personally.

questions.text: When I notice that my university deletes negative feedback, explaining it as protecting its image, I...
options: Think it's normal practice and see nothing wrong with it.
Ask the administration if they could at least analyze the complaints before deleting them.
Suggest responding openly to criticism and using it to fix problems.
Decide not to get involved, thinking it's not my area of influence.

questions.text: When I see recycling bins installed on campus but nobody uses them, I...
options: Suggest adding a short explanation nearby to raise awareness.
Think people just need time to adapt and decide not to interfere.
Ignore it because it's not my initiative or responsibility.
Simply use the bin correctly myself, because personal example matters more than words.

questions.text: If I am assigned to work with someone who is considered difficult by others, I...
options: Stay calm and cooperate without unnecessary emotions.
Try to see their strengths and understand what lies behind their behavior.
Work with them formally, just to complete the task.
Prefer to avoid collaboration to save my nerves.

questions.text: When someone shares a personal story that goes against my values, I...
options: Decide that I shouldn't listen to something that contradicts my beliefs.
Politely change the topic if I feel uncomfortable.
Try to avoid such topics in the future.
Try to listen without judgment to better understand the person.

questions.text: When someone receives fewer opportunities than others, I...
options: Try to understand how to level the playing field or support them.
Believe that if someone didn't get the opportunity, they probably didn't deserve it.
Think the main thing is to focus on my own business.
Think life isn't always fair, but I try to be attentive to people.

questions.text: If I find out that someone is getting advantages through connections, I...
options: Consider that being able to negotiate is also a kind of skill.
Discuss it with others who are also dissatisfied, to see if something can be done.
Think such things have always existed and are part of the system.
Believe it's unfair and at least try to speak openly about it.

questions.text: When I realize that my inaction has caused someone a problem, I...
options: Think that if I didn't do anything, I'm not to blame.
Admit that I'm also responsible, because harm can come from silence.
Believe that everyone is only responsible for their own actions.
Reflect on how I could have acted differently.

questions.text: When someone says the end justifies the means, I...
options: Fully agree, because winners aren't judged.
Partially agree because it depends on the consequences.
Think that sometimes tough decisions are unavoidable.
Believe that if the means cause harm, the goal loses its value.

questions.text: When I think about my city or neighborhood, I...
options: Dream of leaving as soon as possible because nothing will ever change here.
Wish it could become more comfortable and safer.
Think that little depends on me personally.
Want people here to feel they can make changes themselves.

questions.text: When I think about my future, I...
options: Believe that stability and comfort are the most important things.
Want to combine personal success with helping others.
Want to be useful to people, not just earn money.
Think that helping others is a personal choice, not an obligation.

questions.text: When I hear someone criticize our community, I...
options: Think about how to reduce the reasons for criticism.
Just agree because everyone has their opinion, no need to take it personally.
Try to explain that things are not so simple and highlight the positive sides.
Ignore it because criticism always exists and there's no point in trying to change anything.

questions.text: When I notice that someone in the team has withdrawn, I...
options: Try to gently engage them through open discussion.
Approach them directly and ask what's going on, as it's important to bring them back into the process.
Think that not everyone has to be equally active.
Don't pay attention because if they're silent, it means they don't want to participate.

questions.text: When a team discussion reaches a dead end, I...
options: Ask for an outside perspective to get a fresh view.
Suggest identifying exactly where we disagree and moving forward from there.
Propose voting to settle the issue and move on.
Believe there's no point in arguing any further.

questions.text: When my classmates suggest teaming up for a project or competition, I...
options: Think it's easier to do everything myself so I don't depend on others.
Agree to join if I understand how the roles will be distributed.
Feel glad to work together, since collaboration increases our chances of success.
Support the idea but try not to take an active role.

questions.text: When someone in the group doesn't keep their promises, I...
options: Calmly discuss what went wrong and look for ways to rebuild trust.
Decide that I can't trust anyone and it's better to do everything myself.
Feel disappointed but try not to show it.
Bring it up directly but politely, without blaming.

questions.text: When I come across new information, I...
options: Trust it if the source looks reliable.
Sometimes verify it if something feels off.
Think about who said it and why, and try to understand their motives and source.
Don't see the point in checking because you can never know the full truth anyway.

questions.text: When someone points out my mistake, I...
options: Listen carefully, since others can notice what I might have missed.
Assume the person is just being picky.
Try not to pay attention, though I don't like being criticized.
Feel a bit irritated but try to take the feedback constructively.

questions.text: When I'm asked to do something new, I...
options: Refuse, because I don't like feeling inexperienced.
Accept that I might not succeed right away and stay calm about it.
Prefer to stick to what I already know well.
Hope I'll manage, though I worry about possible mistakes.

questions.text: When I think about my future, I...
options: Believe that my efforts will determine what I achieve.
Think that circumstances and luck play the biggest role.
Hope that if I don't give up, things will work out.
Believe success depends more on fate than on personal effort.

questions.text: When I feel envy, I...
options: Acknowledge it and think about what I can do to improve myself.
Think life is unfair and get into a bad mood.
Try not to show that something has affected me.
Shift my focus to something else to calm down.

questions.text: When I feel irritated by others, I...
options: Step aside to cool down a bit.
Try not to show my irritation, even if I'm boiling inside.
Acknowledge my feelings and try to understand what exactly triggered me.
Think irritation is a normal reaction and don't see a reason to overthink it.
